title: Searchable Decision Memory
one_liner: Architectural decisions live as ADRs, versioned, dated and grep-able — not as folklore.
problem: When the rationale behind a structure lives only in someone's head, in a closed Slack thread, or in a deleted Notion page, agents (and engineers) re-derive it badly. They reopen settled debates, undo intentional constraints, or violate a rule that was once written down but is no longer reachable from the code.
solution: Store Architecture Decision Records inside the repo, numbered, dated, with context, decision, consequences and status. Link from the relevant code/READMEs to the ADR. Keep superseded ADRs visible and explicitly marked — the history is part of the memory. An agent asked 'why this way?' should be able to grep an answer.
